engineGuide.h2: Aston Martin DBS Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Aston Martin DBS across classic, modern and Superleggera generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: The Gaydon-era DBS flagship V12, delivering 510hp with naturally aspirated refinement - but coil pack failures risk catastrophic catalyst ingestion.
compatibleVariants: DBS V12, DBS Volante, DBS Carbon Edition, DBS UB-2010, DBS Dragon 88, DBS Zagato Centennial
commonFailures: Coil pack failure at 40k-70k miles causing misfires and potential catalyst ingestion
Timing chain tensioner wear leading to chain noise on high-mileage units
Oil pressure sensor failures triggering warning lights without actual pressure loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM08 Aston Martin DBS engine replacement

entries.title: 5.2L Petrol
entries.history: The Superleggera's twin-turbo V12 producing 715hp, bringing modern performance but introducing turbo and cooling system complexity.
compatibleVariants: DBS Superleggera, DBS Superleggera Volante, DBS Superleggera AMR
commonFailures: Twin turbocharger seal wear causing oil consumption and boost loss on high-mileage examples
Direct injection carbon buildup on intake valves affecting performance on short-journey use
Intercooler and cooling system sensitivity requiring vigilant maintenance in hot climates
entries.cta: Get quotes for AE31 Aston Martin DBS engine replacement

entries.title: 5.3L Petrol
entries.history: The iconic DBS V8 derived from the Lagonda unit, delivering classic Aston character with Weber carburettors and period engineering.
compatibleVariants: DBS V8, DBS Vantage /SVC, DBS Vantage /VC
commonFailures: Cylinder head cracking around valve seats on high-mileage classic aluminium heads
Weber carburettor tuning complexity causing rough running if not set up by specialist
Period rubber oil seal degradation leading to leaks after decades of service
entries.cta: Get quotes for V540 Aston Martin DBS engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: The original DBS powerplant, a Tadek Marek-designed straight-six sharing architecture with the DB6, prized for smoothness and originality.
commonFailures: SU carburettor synchronization issues causing uneven fueling and poor throttle response
Distributor wear affecting ignition timing and causing misfires on original points systems
Cylinder head gasket failures on high-mileage units without recent overhaul
entries.cta: Get quotes for LB Aston Martin DBS engine replacement

entries.title: 5.3L Petrol
entries.history: The ultimate classic DBS Vantage specification with triple Weber carburettors and performance camshaft, delivering 325hp for period enthusiasts.
commonFailures: Triple Weber carburettor synchronization complexity requiring specialist tuning expertise
High-performance camshaft wear accelerating valve train component degradation
Period ignition system limitations causing timing drift under sustained high-rev use
entries.cta: Get quotes for /SVC Aston Martin DBS engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Aston Martin DBS Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Aston Martin DBS spans three distinct eras, with the modern AM08 V12 (2007-2012) dominating replacement enquiries due to coil pack and catalyst ingestion risks, while classic V540 V8 and LB straight-six units face age-related wear. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Coil Pack Failure & Catalyst Ingestion - Why AM08 V12 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DBS V12, DBS Volante, DBS Carbon Edition (2007-2012, AM08)
problems.rootCause: Ignition coil degradation causes misfires, sending unburnt fuel into the catalytic converters which overheat, shatter, and ingest ceramic fragments into the cylinders - destroying the engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 12 ignition coils and spark plugs before misfire symptoms appear
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catalyst damage if done proactively; recommended at 40k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace damaged catalytic converters, clean intake system
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if ceramic fragments haven't entered cylinders; diagnostic essential

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt V12 with upgraded coils, new cats, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Only solution once catalyst ingestion occurs; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: On a DBS worth £40,000-£80,000, preventative coil pack replacement at 40k miles is exceptional value. Once misfires appear, immediate diagnostic is critical - if catalyst ingestion has occurred, engine replacement is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AM08 engine prices for your Aston Martin DBS

problems.h4: Cylinder Head Cracking - Why Classic V540 V8 Engines Need Specialist Rebuilds
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DBS V8, DBS Vantage /SVC (1969-1972, V540)
problems.rootCause: Aluminium cylinder heads develop stress cracks around valve seats due to thermal cycling and period metallurgy, leading to compression loss and coolant contamination.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove heads, weld cracks, machine seats, replace valves and guides
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is sound; requires specialist classic Aston workshop

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine rebuild with new heads, pistons, bearings, seals; matching numbers option
repairOptions.longevity: Essential for concours restoration; includes upgraded components addressing known weaknesses

problems.recommendation: For matching-numbers restorations, a specialist V540 rebuild is non-negotiable. For driver-quality cars, a well-sorted used engine with fresh head work offers better value - but always verify compression and leak-down before purchase.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt V540 engine prices for your Aston Martin DBS

problems.h4: Turbocharger Wear - Why AE31 Superleggera Engines Need Early Attention
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DBS Superleggera, DBS Superleggera Volante (2018-2022, AE31)
problems.rootCause: Twin turbocharger seals degrade under high heat and boost pressure, allowing oil into the intake or exhaust; bearing wear causes shaft play and eventual failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbos, replace seals and gaskets, clean intake/exhaust tracts
repairOptions.longevity: Early intervention option if caught before bearing wear; requires specialist turbo knowledge

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remanufacture or replace both turbochargers with upgraded bearings and seals
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage Superleggeras; addresses root cause of seal failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete V12 rebuild with upgraded turbos, cooling system refresh, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Only solution if turbo failure caused cylinder scoring or oil starvation damage

problems.recommendation: Superleggera owners should monitor boost pressure and oil consumption closely. At first sign of turbo seal weep, specialist inspection is essential - early turbo rebuild is far cheaper than engine replacement after bearing failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AE31 engine prices for your Aston Martin DBS

problems.h4: Carburettor Tuning Complexity - Why Classic DBS Engines Need Specialist Setup
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DBS V8, DBS Vantage /SVC, DBS /VC (1967-1972, V540/LB)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Not mileage-dependent; tuning drift occurs with temperature, altitude, wear
problems.rootCause: Weber 45 DCOE (Vantage) or SU (early DBS) carburettors require precise synchronization and jetting; minor changes cause rough idle, poor throttle response, or fuel wash damaging cylinders.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip, clean, replace worn jets/needles, synchronize on flow bench, dyno tune
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance for classic DBS; recommended every 30k miles or 5 years

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine rebuild with fresh carb setup, upgraded ignition, period-correct components
repairOptions.longevity: Required if poor tuning caused cylinder scoring or piston damage; includes matching numbers option

problems.recommendation: Classic DBS owners should budget for annual carburettor check by a Weber/SU specialist. If the engine runs rough after storage or fuel change, professional setup is cheaper than diagnosing secondary damage from prolonged poor running.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt V540 or LB engine prices for your Aston Martin DBS

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Aston Martin DBS
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you own a classic 1967 straight-six DBS, a modern AM08 V12, or a twin-turbo Superleggera, EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand your engine's unique failure patterns. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original weakness, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes from vetted specialists today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Aston Martin DBS Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Aston Martin DBS and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

groups.title: Aston Martin DBS Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Aston Martin DBS 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The original DBS powerplant from 1967-1969, a Tadek Marek-designed straight-six sharing architecture with the DB6. Prized for smoothness and period character, these engines require specialist SU carburettor expertise and are sought after for matching-numbers restorations.
commonFailurePoints: cylinder head gasket failures on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin DBS 4.0L petrol engines

items.title: Aston Martin DBS 5.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The Superleggera's twin-turbo V12 introduced in 2018, delivering 715hp with modern forced induction. While powerful and refined, the AE31 requires vigilant cooling system maintenance and early attention to turbocharger seal wear to prevent costly failures.
commonFailurePoints: Twin turbocharger seal wear causing oil consumption
direct injection carbon buildup on intake valves
intercooler cooling sensitivity under sustained load
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DBS 5.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Aston Martin DBS 5.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The iconic classic DBS V8 launched in 1969, derived from the Lagonda unit and featuring Weber carburettors on Vantage specifications. Aluminium cylinder heads are prone to stress cracking after decades of thermal cycling, making specialist rebuild expertise essential.
commonFailurePoints: Cylinder head cracking around valve seats on aluminium heads
period rubber oil seal degradation after 50+ years
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DBS 5.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Aston Martin DBS 5.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The Gaydon-era DBS flagship V12 from 2007-2012, naturally aspirated and producing 510hp with characteristic Aston refinement. Coil pack failures pose the greatest risk, as misfires can lead to catastrophic catalyst ingestion if not addressed proactively.
commonFailurePoints: Coil pack failure at 40k-70k miles risking catalyst ingestion
timing chain tensioner wear on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DBS 5.9L petrol engine prices

knownFor: AM08 coil pack failure risking catastrophic catalyst ingestion if misfires ignored

AE31 twin-turbo complexity requiring vigilant cooling system maintenance
All DBS engines prioritise character and refinement over outright efficiency

engineYears.h2: Aston Martin DBS Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Aston Martin DBS production timeline from 1967 to 2022, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Classic DBS Launch: LB Straight-Six Era
years.description: The original DBS launched with Tadek Marek's 4.0L straight-six, sharing architecture with the DB6 and prioritising refinement over outright power.
keyChanges: DBS model launched replacing the DB6 in Aston Martin's GT lineup
LB series straight-six engine introduced with SU carburettors
knownFor: Smooth, refined character typical of Marek-designed six-cylinder units
SU carburettor complexity requiring specialist tuning expertise
Period ignition system limitations affecting high-rev reliability
Highly sought after for matching-numbers concours restorations
years.cta: Get quotes for 1967-1969 Aston Martin DBS replacement engines

years.preview: Classic DBS V8 Introduction: V540 Era
years.description: The iconic V540 V8 replaced the straight-six in 1969, delivering classic Aston character with Weber carburettors and period engineering.
keyChanges: V540 5.3L V8 engine introduced, derived from Lagonda unit
DBS Vantage /SVC specification launched with triple Weber carburettors
Final year of classic DBS production in 1972
knownFor: Aluminium cylinder heads prone to stress cracking after decades of use
Weber carburettor tuning complexity requiring specialist expertise
Period rubber oil seal degradation leading to leaks on high-age units
Matching-numbers preservation critical for concours-value examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 1969-1972 Aston Martin DBS replacement engines
years.ticker: DBS V8 engine rebuild cost • DBS Vantage /SVC engine specialist • V540 cylinder head crack repair

years.preview: Modern DBS Gaydon Era: AM08 V12
years.description: The Gaydon-era DBS returned in 2007 with the naturally aspirated AM08 5.9L V12, delivering 510hp with characteristic Aston refinement.
keyChanges: AM08 5.9L V12 engine introduced, shared with DB9 and V12 Vantage
DBS Volante convertible variant launched in 2009
Limited editions: Carbon Edition (2010), UB-2010 (2010), Dragon 88 (2012), Zagato Centennial (2012)
mainEngines: AM08 - 5.9L petrol (DBS V12, Volante, Carbon Edition, UB-2010, Dragon 88, Zagato Centennial)
knownFor: Coil pack failure at 40k-70k miles risking catastrophic catalyst ingestion
Timing chain tensioner wear on high-mileage units without preventative maintenance
Oil pressure sensor false warnings not indicating actual pressure loss
High replacement demand driven by known failure patterns and specialist rebuild value
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2012 Aston Martin DBS replacement engines
years.ticker: AM08 coil pack replacement cost • DBS V12 catalyst ingestion repair • DBS Volante engine specialist UK

years.preview: DBS Superleggera Era: AE31 Twin-Turbo V12
years.description: The Superleggera returned in 2018 with the AE31 5.2L twin-turbo V12, delivering 715hp with modern forced induction and advanced electronics.
keyChanges: AE31 5.2L twin-turbo V12 engine introduced, exclusive to Superleggera range
DBS Superleggera Volante convertible variant launched in 2019
DBS Superleggera AMR performance specification introduced in 2021
mainEngines: AE31 - 5.2L petrol twin-turbo (DBS Superleggera, Superleggera Volante, Superleggera AMR)
knownFor: Twin turbocharger seal wear causing oil consumption on high-mileage examples
Direct injection carbon buildup on intake valves affecting short-journey driven cars
Cooling system sensitivity requiring vigilant maintenance in hot climates or track use
Lower replacement volume but high-value specialist rebuild demand as cars age out of warranty
years.cta: Enquire about 2018-2022 Aston Martin DBS replacement engines

faq.h2: Aston Martin DBS Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does an Aston Martin DBS engine rebuild cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuild costs vary significantly by generation: classic LB straight-six £12k-£35k, V540 V8 £15k-£45k, modern AM08 V12 £8.5k-£28k, and Superleggera AE31 £18k-£42k, depending on specification and whether matching numbers preservation is required.
keyPoints: AM08 V12 rebuilds start around £8,500 for supply-only rebuilt units
Classic V540 V8 specialist rebuilds command premium pricing due to matching numbers demand
Superleggera AE31 costs reflect twin-turbo complexity and newer component pricing
Supply & fit services add £1,500-£4,500 to engine price depending on variant and location
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Aston Martin DBS engine prices for your variant

items.question: What causes the AM08 V12 engine to fail in the 2007-2012 Aston Martin DBS?
items.answer: Coil pack failure is the primary cause, leading to misfires that send unburnt fuel into the catalytic converters, causing them to overheat, shatter, and ingest ceramic fragments into the cylinders - destroying the engine.
keyPoints: Coil packs typically fail between 40,000-70,000 miles on AM08 V12 engines
Misfire warning lights should trigger immediate diagnostic, not continued driving
Preventative coil pack replacement at 40k miles is far cheaper than engine replacement
Catalyst ingestion is irreversible; once ceramic enters cylinders, engine is scrap
items.cta: Get quotes for AM08 Aston Martin DBS engine replacement

items.question: Is it worth rebuilding a classic Aston Martin DBS V8 engine?
items.answer: For matching-numbers or concours restorations, absolutely - specialist V540 rebuilds preserve provenance and value. For driver-quality cars, a well-sorted used engine with fresh head work may offer better value.
keyPoints: Matching-numbers V540 rebuilds support six-figure restoration values
Cylinder head cracking is the most common failure; specialist welding is essential
Used V540 engines require compression testing and history verification before purchase
Budget £15k-£45k for specialist rebuild depending on specification and originality requirements
items.cta: Compare rebuilt V540 Aston Martin DBS engine prices

items.question: How long does an Aston Martin DBS engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only rebuilt engines typically dispatch within 3-10 working days; supply & fit services require 5-15 working days depending on workshop availability and variant complexity.
keyPoints: Used engines may be available for immediate dispatch if in stock with breakers
Classic V540 and LB rebuilds often have longer lead times due to specialist machining requirements
Superleggera AE31 replacements may require ECU coding and adaptation post-fitment
Always confirm lead times and warranty terms with your chosen supplier before committing
items.cta: Check Aston Martin DBS engine replacement turnaround times

items.question: Can I upgrade my classic DBS engine to Vantage /SVC specification?
items.answer: Yes, but it requires specialist expertise to fit triple Weber carburettors, performance camshaft, and supporting modifications - and may affect originality for concours purposes.
keyPoints: /SVC conversion requires triple Weber 45 DCOE carburettors and synchronization expertise
Performance camshaft and valve train upgrades must be matched to intended use
Conversion may reduce value for matching-numbers concours restoration projects
Budget £18k-£50k for a /SVC-spec rebuild depending on component quality and originality requirements
items.cta: Enquire about DBS Vantage /SVC engine upgrade costs

items.question: What's the most reliable Aston Martin DBS engine?
items.answer: The classic LB straight-six (1967-1969) has the simplest design and fewest catastrophic failure modes, though age-related wear affects all classic units. Modern AM08 V12 has known coil pack risks but is otherwise robust if maintained.
keyPoints: LB straight-six has fewer complex components but requires carburettor expertise
AM08 V12 is reliable if coil packs are replaced preventatively at 40k miles
AE31 twin-turbo Superleggera is newest but turbo complexity adds potential failure points
Regular specialist servicing is more important than engine choice for longevity
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DBS engine reliability and replacement options

items.question: Do I need a specialist to replace my Aston Martin DBS engine?
items.answer: Yes - DBS engines require model-specific knowledge for ancillary fitment, ECU coding, and commissioning. Generalist workshops risk incorrect setup causing premature failure.
keyPoints: AM08 V12 requires coil pack programming and catalyst adaptation post-fitment
Classic V540 engines need Weber carburettor synchronization by experienced specialists
AE31 Superleggera requires twin-turbo system calibration and cooling system verification
EngineMarket's vetted network ensures DBS-specialist expertise for your replacement
items.cta: Find Aston Martin DBS engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Aston Martin DBS engine?
items.answer: Engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects in the rebuild.
keyPoints: Warranty terms vary by supplier; always confirm exact coverage before purchase
Unlimited mileage warranties are standard for rebuilt units through our network
Warranty typically excludes damage from improper installation, maintenance neglect, or misuse
Keep all service records to maintain warranty validity post-installation
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DBS rebuilt engine warranty terms

items.question: Can I use a used Aston Martin DBS engine as a replacement?
items.answer: Yes, used engines offer cost-effective replacement when sourced from reputable breakers with full history, compression test results, and verification of no catalyst ingestion or cylinder scoring.
keyPoints: Always request compression test results and service history before purchasing used
Verify engine code matches your variant to avoid compatibility issues
Budget for preventative maintenance (coils, seals, gaskets) on installation regardless of mileage
Used engines typically carry shorter warranty terms than rebuilt units
items.cta: Check used Aston Martin DBS engine availability and pricing

items.question: How do I find my Aston Martin DBS engine code?
items.answer: Enter your registration number on EngineMarket to instantly identify your exact engine code via DVLA data, or locate the code stamped on the engine block near the timing cover or cylinder head.
keyPoints: AM08 codes are stamped on the front timing cover of the V12
V540 codes appear on the left cylinder head near the distributor
LB codes are found on the engine block adjacent to the oil filter housing
DVLA lookup via registration is the fastest, most reliable identification method
items.cta: Identify your Aston Martin DBS engine code instantly via registration lookup
